cards.text: 40 years, zero ground broken on main Kampung Baru — even as Kampung Sungai Baru sub-project finally moved forward in 2025.
cards.sub: PKB's master plan envisages ~RM43 billion across 121 hectares with 17,500 residential units. Fragmented Malay Reserve Land ownership slows consolidation.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: 121 hectares of Kampung Baru sit beside the KLCC core — economically prime but legally complicated by Malay Reserve Land status.
cards.s: Land in Kampung Baru is some of the most valuable in KL on a per-square-foot basis, yet the master plan has not produced a single completed redevelopment block.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Thousands of Malay families have lived in Kampung Baru for generations — any redevelopment means displacing a historic community.
cards.s: Cultural heritage value is real and is the central reason consolidation has been politically difficult across multiple administrations.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: PKB (Perbadanan Pembangunan Kampong Bharu), set up in 2012, has not delivered the master redevelopment despite years of operational spending.
cards.s: Malay Reserve Land status creates legal complexity requiring individual consent from a large number of fragmented lot owners.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Kampung Baru is not a real estate problem. It is the unresolved question of whether Malay heritage and KLCC-grade land economics can coexist in the same blocks.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like a historic building where the heirs cannot agree on renovation — 40 years of blueprints but the walls keep crumbling.
cards.sub: Fragmented ownership and competing visions have paralysed redevelopment for four decades. The Kg Sungai Baru breakthrough shows it can move when terms are right.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: The path forward is not a bigger plan — it is structured smaller-block consolidation like Kg Sungai Baru, with cultural heritage protections built into each phase.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: Kampung Baru Master Redevelopment Still Stalled After 40 Years
